transcript: The binary search has a logarithmic time complexity of all log n and a constant space complexity of O. It is efficiently found that target is sorted array by dividing the search interval in half of each step, making it much faster than linear search as described on code camp. By the best case occurs when the middle element is the target on the first try and the worst case occurs when the search continues until the array is reduced to one element. In O1 iterative, no extra memory is used, but in O log n recursive due to recursion stack, it is used.
